<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <!-- Documentation generated for every build, even during local dev -->
    <GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Analyzers always on, even for dev inner loop -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <AnalysisLevel>latest-recommended</AnalysisLevel>

  </PropertyGroup>

<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Only generate XML docs in CI / Release builds -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(Configuration)' == 'Release' or '$(CI)' == 'true'">
    <GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Analyzers: always enforce in CI/Release, skip in Debug inner loop -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <AnalysisLevel>latest-recommended</AnalysisLevel>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <NoWarn>$(NoWarn);CS1591;CA1000</NoWarn>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(Configuration)' == 'Release' or '$(CI)' == 'true'">
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(Configuration)' != 'Release' and '$(CI)' != 'true'">
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>false</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Significant improvement: ~19s → ~7s for incremental build by disabling analyzers and doc generation. That's about 60% faster.

Now let me also check the redundant project reference impact. Let me also check if the TargetFramework is duplicated in both Directory.Build.props and individual csproj files.

Let me summarize all the issues:

1. **Analyzers always on in inner loop** (`EnforceCodeStyleInBuild=true`): This forces code style analyzers to run on every compilation, even for dev inner-loop builds. Impact: ~12s overhead on no-change rebuild.


2. **XML documentation generated every build** (`GenerateDocumentationFile=true`): Generates XML docs even during development. Only needed for CI/Release builds.
3. **Redundant transitive project references**: BusinessLogic unnecessarily references Common when DataAccess already depends on it, WebApi pulls in DataAccess and Common directly when only BusinessLogic is needed, and Tests references multiple projects transitively when it only needs WebApi. This creates extra dependency resolution work and reduces build parallelism.
4. **Duplicate TargetFramework declarations**: Each project specifies `net8.0` individually even though it's already centralized in Directory.Build.props, creating unnecessary maintenance overhead. The solution also defines unused x64 and x86 platform configurations alongside "Any CPU" when all projects target only "Any CPU", adding clutter and slowing Visual Studio operations. Additionally, the solution file uses outdated C# project type GUIDs instead of the modern SDK-style format.
